source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A student named Xiaozan designed an experimental setup to measure ambient temperature using an electronic balance. A thermally conductive cylinder, open at the top, is fixed on a table. A piston with mass $m_{1}=600\mathrm{{g}}$ and cross-sectional area $S=20\mathrm{cm}^{2}$ seals a certain mass of ideal gas within the cylinder. The piston moves without friction against the cylinder wall. A light, rigid rod is supported at its center by a fixed pivot point A. The left end of the rod is vertically connected to the piston via an inextensible string, while the right end is vertically connected to an iron block of mass $m_{2}=1200\mathrm{g}$, which rests on the electronic balance. When the electronic balance reads $600.0\mathrm{g}$, the measured ambient temperature is $T_{1}=300\mathrm{K}$. Given the external atmospheric pressure $p_{0}=1.0{\times}10^{5}\mathrm{Pa}$ and the acceleration due to gravity $g=10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.

1. When the electronic balance reads $400.0\mathrm{g}$, what is the ambient temperature $T_{2}$?
2. What is the maximum ambient temperature $T_{\mathrm{max}}$ that this device can measure?

issues: Top-level image_paths and metadata.image_paths are inconsistent.

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate substantial visual reliance.
The problem describes a specific apparatus with spatial/mechanical relations (piston, centered pivot rod, left/right connections, balance) that are typically conveyed by the diagram.
An image asset is present and registry_passed=true, so the visual component is available for use.
multi_step: There are two explicit subquestions: solving for T2 at a new balance reading and determining the maximum measurable temperature Tmax.
The setup requires combining multiple conditions from mechanics and thermodynamics rather than a single lookup or one-step computation.
The long question text and apparatus description suggest multi-condition reasoning over force balance and gas-state relations.
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and answer_text contains two concise numeric results ('297K' and '309K').
The quantities asked for are well-defined physical values, making downstream rule-based or physics-based checking feasible.
registry_passed=true, question_present=true, and answer_present=true support reliable evaluation.

risk_flags: Top-level image_paths and metadata.image_paths are inconsistent.

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和主图像资产均存在，核心语义完整，可直接进入候选池。
initial scoring 显示 image_dependency、multi_step 和 verifiability 均较高，样本质量和研究价值较好。
虽存在 image_paths 元数据不一致问题，但注册已通过且实际图像文件存在，不构成核心语义缺失。

variants.rewritten_question_text:
A student named Xiaozan designed an experimental setup to measure ambient temperature using an electronic balance. A thermally conductive cylinder, open at the top, is fixed on a table. A piston with mass $m_{1}=600\mathrm{g}$ and cross-sectional area $S=20\mathrm{cm}^{2}$ seals a certain mass of ideal gas within the cylinder. The piston moves without friction against the cylinder wall. A light, rigid rod is supported at its center by a fixed pivot point A. The left end of the rod is vertically connected to the piston via an inextensible string, while the right end is vertically connected to an iron block of mass $m_{2}=1200\mathrm{g}$, which rests on the electronic balance. When the electronic balance reads $600.0\mathrm{g}$, the measured ambient temperature is $T_{1}=300\mathrm{K}$. Given the external atmospheric pressure $p_{0}=1.0\times10^{5}\mathrm{Pa}$ and the acceleration due to gravity $g=10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.

1. When the electronic balance reads $400.0\mathrm{g}$, what is the ambient temperature $T_{2}$?
2. What is the maximum ambient temperature $T_{\mathrm{max}}$ that this device can measure?

open_ended_problem_variants.title: PhysReason 开放题
open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text:
A student named Xiaozan designed an experimental setup to measure ambient temperature using an electronic balance. A thermally conductive cylinder, open at the top, is fixed on a table. A piston with mass $m_{1}=600\mathrm{g}$ and cross-sectional area $S=20\mathrm{cm}^{2}$ seals a certain mass of ideal gas within the cylinder. The piston moves without friction against the cylinder wall. A light, rigid rod is supported at its center by a fixed pivot point A. The left end of the rod is vertically connected to the piston via an inextensible string, while the right end is vertically connected to an iron block of mass $m_{2}=1200\mathrm{g}$, which rests on the electronic balance. When the electronic balance reads $600.0\mathrm{g}$, the measured ambient temperature is $T_{1}=300\mathrm{K}$. Given the external atmospheric pressure $p_{0}=1.0\times10^{5}\mathrm{Pa}$ and the acceleration due to gravity $g=10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.

1. When the electronic balance reads $400.0\mathrm{g}$, what is the ambient temperature $T_{2}$?
2. What is the maximum ambient temperature $T_{\mathrm{max}}$ that this device can measure?
